key_findings: event_id SHA-256 (tool|kind|asset|value|port|source_file|line_no) is already the correct ClickHouse deduplication key — maps directly to SQLite PRIMARY KEY
ReplacingMergeTree(ingested_at) ORDER BY (event_id) provides idempotent replay without per-row existence checks, reducing sink worker complexity significantly
BEGIN IMMEDIATE claim pattern from WO-00022 must be reused for sink_outbox batch claiming — prevents double-delivery under concurrent workers
CLAIMED transient status is required for crash recovery — stale CLAIMED rows must be reaped back to PENDING on worker startup (same pattern as reclaim_stale_leases in WO-00022)
ClickHouse does not raise uniqueness errors on ReplacingMergeTree duplicate inserts — ALREADY_SYNCED classification from WO-00023 taxonomy is not needed for CH; all inserts succeed immediately
clickhouse-driver native protocol (port 9000) is 3-5x faster than HTTP for bulk inserts; matches the DATABASE_OPS.md host/port (127.0.0.1:9000)
LowCardinality(String) for tool and event_kind fields yields ~10x compression on 4-value fields; important for 10M+ row scale
raw_json column must be capped at 1MB per row to avoid hitting ClickHouse max_query_size limits on bulk inserts

decisions.rationale: Canonical ClickHouse idempotency pattern; deduplicates by event_id on background merge; no per-row checks needed; FINAL modifier resolves at query time
event_id SHA-256 is globally unique; enables O(log N) point lookups and deduplication; matches SQLite PK for natural join
SQLite WAL serializes concurrent writes; IMMEDIATE acquires reserved lock at transaction start; prevents double-claim across workers

decisions.decision: CLAIMED transient status for in-flight rows
decisions.rationale: Tracks which rows a specific worker has claimed; enables targeted reaping on crash without touching PENDING/FAILED rows from other workers
Native protocol bulk insert throughput 3-5x HTTP; supports streaming blocks; matches DATABASE_OPS.md port 9000 configuration
500 × 60s = 30k events/min throughput; ClickHouse handles 500-row blocks efficiently; leaves CPU headroom for concurrent scan loop

risk_model.risk: ClickHouse config directory wipe (known DATABASE_OPS.md failure pattern)
risk_model.mitigation: Worker detects health check failure before claiming batch; rows remain PENDING until CH recovers; runbook: apt-get install --reinstall clickhouse-server

risk_model.risk: ReplacingMergeTree dedup is asynchronous — temporary duplicates between merges
risk_model.mitigation: Use SELECT ... FINAL for all integrity-check queries; documented in WO-00024 monitoring spec MON-03

risk_model.risk: raw_json field may contain arbitrarily large JSON blobs
risk_model.mitigation: Cap at 1MB per row pre-insert; log warning when truncation occurs

risk_model.risk: Backfill of 4.6M existing rows may saturate ClickHouse during initial catch-up
risk_model.mitigation: Rate-limit backfill: batch-size 500, interval 2s; interleaves with scan data; completes in ~3h at 250k/min

risk_model.risk: CLAIMED rows not reaped if reaper interval is too long after crash
risk_model.mitigation: Reaper runs at every worker startup and every 10 sweeps; CLAIM_TIMEOUT_S=600 (10min)

recommended_actions: Create pandavs_recon.scan_events table with ReplacingMergeTree DDL before deploying worker
Deploy clickhouse_sink_worker.py with run-once for initial backfill of 4.6M existing outbox rows
Then switch to loop mode (--interval 60) for continuous sync
Run SELECT count() FROM pandavs_recon.scan_events FINAL after backfill to verify count matches SQLite events count
Integrate worker status into WO-00024 hourly report (MON-03 query reads event_sync_ledger)

cross_wo_dependencies.WO-00022: BEGIN IMMEDIATE claim pattern reused from chunk_queue design
cross_wo_dependencies.WO-00023: sink_outbox, event_sync_ledger, dead_letter_events, sink_outbox_retry_log tables required
cross_wo_dependencies.WO-00024: event_sync_ledger.sink_target='clickhouse' feeds MON-03 hourly report; ALT-04/ALT-05 alert on outbox lag/failure
